subtopic_summary.objective: Use gas exchange, blood flow, renal transport, pH/CO2, and pressure-volume logic to predict homeostasis.
Set up genotypes, gametes, dominance, recessiveness, and conditional probability before choosing an answer.
Use population models, community interactions, biodiversity metrics, and ecological reasoning.
Compare viruses, bacteria, fungi, protists, plasmids, antibiotics, conjugation, and host-pathogen interactions.
Separate selection mode, adaptation, isolation, gene flow, speciation, and evolutionary evidence.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Plant reproduction, development and life cycles
subtopic_summary.objective: Track ploidy, generations, flowers, endosperm, seeds, fruits, sporophytes, and gametophytes.
Predict nerve, sensory, muscle, synapse, and action-potential effects from mechanism.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Plant tissues, xylem/phloem and water transport
subtopic_summary.objective: Relate plant structure to xylem/phloem flow, source-sink logic, stomata, tissues, and water potential.
Map endocrine axes, hormone source, target, feedback, and homeostatic response.
Recognize biomolecule classes, carbohydrates/lipids/nucleotides, pH chemistry, and reagent-test evidence.
Connect fertilization, cleavage, germ layers, embryonic structures, developmental signals, and reproductive physiology.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Cellular respiration, ETC and ATP synthesis
subtopic_summary.objective: Trace electrons, proton gradients, ATP synthase, uncouplers, fermentation, and cellular energy yield.
Separate innate behavior, habituation, conditioning, imprinting, kin selection, and proximate/ultimate explanations.
Match organs, digestive enzymes, nutrient absorption, vitamins/minerals, and deficiency symptoms.
Map DNA to RNA to protein, including promoters, operators, RNA processing, codons, ribosomes, and regulatory elements.
Read tree topology and distinguish common ancestry from superficial similarity or taxonomy rank.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Protein structure, amino acids and enzymes
subtopic_summary.objective: Connect amino-acid chemistry, folding, enzyme behavior, allostery, and protein function.
Explain pigments, light reactions, Calvin cycle, photosystems, C3/C4/CAM logic, and action spectra.
Distinguish innate/adaptive immunity, antibody classes, inflammation, immune cells, and pathogen recognition.
Explain replication machinery, chromosome structure, origins, telomeres, and replication errors.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Lab methods, biotechnology and molecular tools
subtopic_summary.objective: Know what PCR, blots, gels, sequencing, plasmid maps, microarrays, CRISPR, and probes can show.
subtopic_summary.template_archetype: PCR / blot / gel / sequencing method choice

subtopic_summary.objective: Trace energy, biomass, productivity, trophic efficiency, and elemental cycles.
Eliminate autosomal/X-linked and dominant/recessive modes using pedigree constraints.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Membrane transport, osmosis and electrochemical gradients
subtopic_summary.objective: Use gradients, channels, pumps, transporters, and membrane potentials to predict movement and physiological effect.
Assign cell functions to organelles, cytoskeletal systems, vesicles, and intracellular compartments.
Use allele frequencies, carrier frequencies, and equilibrium assumptions to predict genotype proportions.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Cell cycle, meiosis and cancer checkpoints
subtopic_summary.objective: Predict effects of cell-cycle state, mitosis/meiosis errors, cyclins, oncogenes, tumor suppressors, and checkpoints.
Predict permeability and membrane behavior from lipid composition, saturation, hydrophobicity, and bilayer structure.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Plant hormones, tropisms and environmental responses
subtopic_summary.objective: Predict plant growth and germination from auxin, ethylene, gibberellin, ABA, phytochrome, and environmental cues.

subtopic_summary.subtopic: Cell signaling, receptors and second messengers
subtopic_summary.objective: Trace ligand-receptor binding through second messengers, kinases, and cellular response.
Identify parental/recombinant classes, map order, double crossovers, and recombination distance.
Infer locus number, additive effects, and phenotype frequencies from trait distributions.
